Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Against Plaintiff And Plaintiffs Counsel Of Record
Off Calendar Jul-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT OSCAR LAZO CORTEZ'S Motion To Dismiss Against Plaintiff And Plaintiffs Counsel Of Record is Transferred to be heard at 9:00 a.m. on July 12, 2017 in courtroom 301 on the discovery calendar. Defendant seeks a dismissal as a discovery sanction. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/SRB)

Defendant Oscar Lazo Cortez'S Motion To Dismiss Against Plaintiff And Plaintiffs Counsel Of Record
Off Calendar Jul-12-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Ruling: Defendant Oscar Lazo Cortez's Motion To Dismiss Against Plaintiff And Plaintiffs Counsel Of Record is GRANTED. On December 13, 2016, the Court granted Cortez' Motion to Compel Discovery Responses and Request for Reasonable Sanctions and Attorneys' Fees (Blair Decl., Exhibit 11). On March 16, 2017, the Court ordered the Defendant's Requests for Admission, Set One, served on October 28, 2016, deemed admitted and that "Plaintiff Jerome Butler is precluded from offering testimony at trial and/or submitting testimony by declaration at trial". (Blair Decl., Exhibit 13). As of June 5, 2017, plaintiff has failed to comply with the Court's December 13, 2016 order. (Blair Decl., par. 21). Plaintiff failed to oppose this Motion. When a party persists in disobeying a court's orders, the sanctions of dismissing the action are justified. Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771, 796. Plaintiff's Complaint is hereby dismissed. Defendant's counsel shall serve this order via express mail on all parties. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-28-2017 Continued To Oct-03-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Thuy M. Le, Esq., of Law Offices of Brian L. Larsen appearing for Plaintiff. The OSC is re-set to October 3, 2017 at 10:30 am, in Department 610. Judge Newton Lam.; Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos; Proceedings not reported. (610)
